summary: Finish the Liepin and OpenCLI boundary wrapper split while carrying the local Codex intake harness and Liepin detail-target configuration changes.
rationale: This PR intentionally crosses provider, runtime, frontend, governance, docs, tests, and local-harness files because the OpenCLI/Liepin split must remain coherent with the already-local main changes the branch is absorbing. The boundary wrapper work keeps OpenCLI command/runtime details inside the OpenCLI automation layer and keeps Liepin DOM/UI behavior inside the Liepin site adapter.
config_behavior_rationale: The new Liepin exploit/explore detail-target settings are consumed by the runtime/provider behavior carried in this same integration PR, so the config/env changes are reviewed as part of the source-decoupling manifest rather than hidden in a separate behavior change.
line_count_rationale: These files were already over the normal line budget, or the new adapter intentionally wraps an existing oversized browser workflow for a source-boundary split. The changes here are bounded to compatibility, boundary wrappers, governance review metadata, and focused tests rather than opportunistic file splitting.
verification: uv run --group dev ty check src tests tools
uv run ruff check src tests tools
deletion_targets: Liepin UI text and DOM behavior inside OpenCLI runtime modules
source-coupled local harness setup notes once they are promoted to canonical docs
risks: The Liepin site adapter is intentionally large in this PR because it preserves behavior while isolating browser automation contracts; later cleanup should split it only after the boundary is stable.
The config/env plus behavior change is explicitly reviewed here because the local main changes were already present and are being folded into the same integration PR.
The governance rule change must remain narrow: major refactor PRs still need to list config_env_files and explain why behavior changes are bundled.
